expectations.text: problems.md explicitly names 'utils.py' as a dumping-ground / generic filename problem
expectations.evidence: Section 1 'Dumping-ground filename' explicitly calls out utils.py as a banlisted dumping-ground name.

expectations.text: problems.md calls out the generic class name 'Manager' as a naming problem
expectations.evidence: Section 2 lists 'Manager — on the banlist; manages nothing specific' and 'Manager.do_stuff' as banlist names.

expectations.text: problems.md identifies __getattr__ / dynamic dispatch as a problem for AI agents
expectations.evidence: Section 4 explicitly calls out DynamicAPI.__getattr__ returning a generic lambda, making callers invisible to grep.

expectations.text: problems.md flags the use of eval() as an anti-pattern
expectations.evidence: Section 4 flags 'eval(expr) in run_expression — dynamic code execution; also a security hazard.'

expectations.text: problems.md identifies the deep inheritance chain
expectations.evidence: Section 5 'Deep inheritance chain' explicitly describes User -> Auditable -> Persisted -> Entity -> Base as 4 levels deep.

expectations.text: problems.md notes that public functions lack type annotations / return types
expectations.evidence: Section 3 'Missing type annotations at the public boundary' enumerates each untyped function.

expectations.text: refactored.py's public functions have type annotations on parameters and return values
expectations.evidence: The sole public symbol User dataclass has typed fields (id: str, email: str); no untyped functions exist.

expectations.text: refactored.py does not use __getattr__ for runtime method dispatch
expectations.evidence: refactored.py contains no __getattr__ implementation; only a frozen/slotted User dataclass.

expectations.text: refactored.py does not call eval() or exec() on user input
expectations.evidence: No eval or exec calls appear anywhere in refactored.py.

expectations.text: refactored.py replaces the generic 'Manager' class name with something domain-specific (or removes it)
expectations.evidence: Manager is removed entirely, replaced by the domain-specific User class.

expectations.text: refactored.py's inheritance chain is at most 2 levels deep (or uses composition)
expectations.evidence: User is a flat dataclass inheriting only from object (depth 1).

expectations.text: rationale.md grounds recommendations in specific AI-agent failure modes rather than generic clean-code arguments
expectations.evidence: Rationale explicitly cites agent hallucination of MRO, grep-visibility for agents, and agents importing private helpers.

expectations.text: refactored.py declares __all__ to make the public surface explicit (v1.3 guidance — Python-specific)
expectations.evidence: refactored.py line 25 declares __all__ = ["User"].

expectations.evidence: User is declared @dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True).

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md gives a concrete file-size guideline with a number
expectations.evidence: Section 2 states 'Hard cap: 300 lines per file. Soft target: 150.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md requires type annotations on public / module-boundary functions
expectations.evidence: Section 4 requires 'Typed public signatures everywhere. No any' with strict and noImplicitAny enabled.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md mentions tests/lint/typecheck as the verification loop
expectations.evidence: Section 8 defines 'bun run check runs bun typecheck && bun lint && bun test' as the agent's feedback loop.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md discourages dynamic/metaprogramming patterns for agent-traceability
expectations.evidence: Section 5 bans Proxy, Reflect, runtime monkey-patching, and decorator stacks because 'Agents can't grep through them.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md explains at least one decision in terms of AI-agent behavior
expectations.evidence: Rationale repeatedly cited, e.g., 'Files over 300 regularly break agent apply-model merges' and 'Agent memory of an API is version-specific.'

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md recommends branded / nominal types for IDs (e.g. UserId as branded string) — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 4 specifies 'type UserId = string & { readonly __brand: UserId }' and forbids passing raw string where an ID is meant.

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md addresses dependency pinning, version pinning, or 'boring stack' / training-data hygiene — v1.3 guidance

expectations.text: CONVENTIONS.md explicitly discourages barrel / re-export index files — v1.3 guidance (AR011)
expectations.evidence: Section 13: 'Don't add index.ts barrels except in db/schema/.'

expectations.text: recommendations.md discusses naming with explicit reference to grep-ability, retrieval, or localization
expectations.evidence: Section 4 says 'Agents grep for createUser and find it in utils.ts... They edit the wrong one.'

expectations.text: recommendations.md recommends organizing code by feature / vertical slice rather than horizontal layer
expectations.evidence: Section 3 advocates flat feature files and keeping route, validation, handler, and types within 1-2 files.

expectations.text: recommendations.md warns against metaprogramming, reflection, or dynamic dispatch
expectations.evidence: Section 17 'Avoid magic metaprogramming' warns against decorators, Proxy-based ORMs, and plugin transforms.

expectations.text: recommendations.md recommends typed public boundaries / type annotations at module seams
expectations.evidence: Section 2 'Make the type system the source of truth' plus Section 16 JSDoc on public APIs.

expectations.text: recommendations.md recommends verification affordances tied to preventing agent hallucination spirals
expectations.evidence: Section 12 mentions one validation command but does not tie verification to hallucination spirals or wrong-patch loops.

expectations.text: recommendations.md addresses file size, near-duplicates, or str_replace breakage
expectations.evidence: Section 8 targets 5-40 line functions and flags 300-line functions as a failure mode, though does not mention str_replace.

expectations.text: recommendations.md grounds reasoning in specific agent failure modes rather than generic clean-code platitudes
expectations.evidence: Failure modes are mostly generic ('agent picks one uniformly', 'agent will invent'); lacks concrete context window, str_replace, lost-in-the-middle, or documented spiral citations.

expectations.text: recommendations.md presents the list in a prioritized order
expectations.evidence: Explicit P0/P1/P2 legend with sections ordered accordingly.

expectations.text: recommendations.md covers deterministic tests (seeded randomness, frozen time) OR injectable side effects — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 19 prescribes seeded randomness, fixture factories, and injecting a clock instead of Date.now().

expectations.text: recommendations.md mentions branded/nominal types OR discriminated unions over magic strings — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 22 'Make failure states first-class' uses discriminated union {ok:true} | {ok:false} pattern; Section 7 uses exhaustive switches on unions.

expectations.text: recommendations.md flags barrel re-export files or 'export *' as an anti-pattern — v1.3 guidance (AR011)
expectations.evidence: Section 4 explicitly advises avoiding barrel index.ts re-exports across the whole app.

expectations.text: recommendations.md mentions dependency pinning OR training-data / 'boring stack' considerations — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 11 'Commit lockfiles' covers dependency pinning via lockfiles.

expectations.text: recommendations.md discusses naming with explicit reference to grep-ability, retrieval, or localization
expectations.evidence: Section 1 says 'Agents search by filename and pattern' and Section 4 prescribes greppable names.

expectations.text: recommendations.md recommends organizing code by feature / vertical slice rather than horizontal layer
expectations.evidence: Section 1 shows src/users/ feature directory and Section 6 says 'Split by feature, not by layer within a feature.'

expectations.text: recommendations.md warns against metaprogramming, reflection, or dynamic dispatch
expectations.evidence: Section 13 'Prefer plain data structures over clever abstractions' warns against decorators, Proxy, and runtime metaprogramming.

expectations.text: recommendations.md recommends typed public boundaries / type annotations at module seams
expectations.evidence: Section 2 colocates contracts with implementations and Section 17 mandates schemas at boundaries; Section 20 requires strict TypeScript.

expectations.text: recommendations.md recommends verification affordances tied to preventing agent hallucination spirals
expectations.evidence: Section 12 'Make how to run it trivial' addresses tool commands but does not tie verification loops to preventing hallucination spirals or wrong-patch loops.

expectations.text: recommendations.md addresses file size, near-duplicates, or str_replace breakage
expectations.evidence: Section 6 soft-caps files at 300 lines and flags the agent-missing-cross-references failure, though does not name str_replace.

expectations.text: recommendations.md grounds reasoning in specific agent failure modes rather than generic clean-code platitudes
expectations.evidence: Failure modes are described generically ('agent patches wrong layer', 'hallucinate type signatures') without naming context-window limits, str_replace, lost-in-the-middle, or specific documented spirals.

expectations.text: recommendations.md presents the list in a prioritized order
expectations.evidence: Explicit Tier 1 through Tier 4 ordering by impact.

expectations.text: recommendations.md covers deterministic tests (seeded randomness, frozen time) OR injectable side effects — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 22 mentions seeds and fixtures in passing but does not prescribe seeded randomness, frozen time, or injectable clocks/side effects for deterministic tests.

expectations.text: recommendations.md mentions branded/nominal types OR discriminated unions over magic strings — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: No mention of branded/nominal types; discriminated unions are not recommended as an alternative to magic strings anywhere in the document.

expectations.text: recommendations.md flags barrel re-export files or 'export *' as an anti-pattern — v1.3 guidance (AR011)
expectations.evidence: Section 7 caps barrel files at one level and prefers direct imports over barrel index.ts re-exports.

expectations.text: recommendations.md mentions dependency pinning OR training-data / 'boring stack' considerations — v1.3 guidance
expectations.evidence: Section 16 'Pin and document external contracts' explicitly pins exact versions for network-boundary packages.
